_comment: Claude Desktop MCP configuration for SCX (Pacemaker v2).
_instructions: 1. Build the server first: cd scx-mcp-server && npm install && npm run build
2. Copy this file's mcpServers block into your Claude Desktop config:
3. Replace all placeholder values (YOUR_*) with real values

scx._comment: Local development — stdio transport, runs as child process

scx-dev-tsx._comment: Alternative: run TypeScript directly without build step (requires tsx installed)

scx-production._comment: Production — HTTP transport, points to deployed MCP server

_env_reference.SCX_API_URL: Base URL of forecast-backend (e.g. https://api.pacemaker.ai or http://localhost:3000)
_env_reference.SCX_TENANT_ID: Your company/tenant ID — used for SSE stream subscription
_env_reference.SCX_API_KEY: API key for endpoints that accept x-api-key (event-extraction, commodity-price/import)
_env_reference.SCX_JWT_TOKEN: JWT Bearer token — obtain from Keycloak or set MCP_CLIENT_* for CC flow
_env_reference.KEYCLOAK_URL: Keycloak base URL for token refresh (e.g. https://auth.pacemaker.ai)
_env_reference.SCX_KEYCLOAK_REALM: Keycloak realm name for your tenant
_env_reference.MCP_CLIENT_ID: Keycloak client ID for the MCP service account
_env_reference.MCP_CLIENT_SECRET: Keycloak client secret for the MCP service account

_security_notes: Never commit this file with real credentials to version control
Use a dedicated MCP service account in Keycloak with minimum required roles
For Group A+B (read-only), the Standard role is sufficient
For Group C (execute_forecast) and Group E (import), Admin role is required
Rotate API keys regularly and store them in a secrets manager for production
